slides.title: A warm sauna may be *quietly good* for the heart.
slides.sub: Regular heat is a gentle stress your body adapts to. Research links it to cardiovascular health.

slides.title: Heat as a gentle stressor
slides.body: Warmth nudges your heart and vessels to work, then recover. Your body *adapts* to that mild, repeated challenge.

slides.title: Tied to a healthy heart
slides.body: Regular sauna use *is linked to* better cardiovascular markers in long observational studies. It is association, not proof.

slides.title: Frequency seems to matter
slides.body: In the studies, more sessions a week tracked with the strongest links. A few relaxed sessions *may support* this.

slides.title: Some should go gently
slides.body: Heat isn't for everyone. If you are pregnant or have heart concerns, *check with a doctor* first.

slides.title: A calm, warming habit
slides.body: Sauna is no magic cure, but a relaxing one with promising links. Hydrate, ease in, and enjoy it.

slides.title: Build calm into *your week*.
slides.body: Vibe Health helps you track the recovery, sleep and movement habits that support a healthy heart, all in one daily score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
A warm sauna may be quietly good for your heart, and it feels wonderful too. 🌿

Heat is a gentle stress your body adapts to, and regular use is linked to better cardiovascular markers. Ease in, hydrate, and check with a doctor if you have concerns. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your recovery habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: How Regular Sauna Use Is Linked to Heart Health
